rules: This report is the XT-side source truth consumed by the generic xhub doctor export.
readyForFirstTask may only be true when hubReachability, modelRouteReadiness, bridgeToolReadiness, and sessionRuntimeReadiness are all ready.
consumedContracts should carry the frozen xt.unified_doctor_report_contract.v1 identifier rather than repeating the report schemaVersion as if it were an upstream dependency.
projectContextPresentation is currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can project project context assembly diagnostics.

hubMemoryPromptProjection is currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can surface the Hub-provided prompt assembly projection for the latest governed coding turn without local prompt re-inference.
projectGovernanceRuntimeReadinessProjection is currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can expose the structured A-tier configured-vs-runtime-ready split without reparsing detailLines.

projectMemoryPolicyProjection, projectMemoryReadinessProjection, and projectMemoryAssemblyResolutionProjection are currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can expose role-aware Project AI memory policy, readiness, and effective assembly resolution as first-class machine-readable source truth.

heartbeatGovernanceProjection is currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can project heartbeat-governed review cadence, quality, anomaly, next-review-due truth, and any attached Project AI memory readiness signal used for review explainability.

skillDoctorTruthProjection is currently expected on skillsCompatibilityReadiness when XT can expose the project effective skill profile snapshot plus typed per-skill readiness counts/previews as first-class machine-readable doctor truth.

supervisorMemoryPolicyProjection and supervisorMemoryAssemblyResolutionProjection are currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can expose role-aware Supervisor review-memory policy and effective assembly resolution as first-class machine-readable source truth.

supervisorReviewTriggerProjection is currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can expose Supervisor review policy mode, effective trigger set, active heartbeat-derived review candidate, queue posture, and latest review-note source as first-class machine-readable doctor truth.

supervisorGuidanceContinuityProjection is currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can expose Supervisor latest-review/latest-guidance/pending-ack guidance continuity plus safe-point and ack posture as first-class machine-readable doctor truth.

supervisorSafePointTimelineProjection is currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can expose pending guidance delivery timing, live safe-point posture, and tool-batch pause posture as first-class machine-readable doctor truth.

projectRemoteSnapshotCacheProjection and supervisorRemoteSnapshotCacheProjection are currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can expose project/supervisor remote snapshot cache provenance without reparsing detailLines.

providerKeySelectionProjection is currently expected on modelRouteReadiness when XT can replay the latest real remote provider-key routing decision for the requested model without re-deriving scheduler truth locally.

providerKeyRouteContextProjection is currently expected on modelRouteReadiness when XT needs one structured route-context envelope carrying the requested model, live provider-pool snapshot, latest selection decision, and any provider-key import-source blockers without reparsing detailLines per surface.
memoryRouteTruthProjection is currently expected on modelRouteReadiness when XT can project upstream memory route diagnostics.
durableCandidateMirrorProjection is currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT has supervisor durable-candidate mirror evidence to surface.
localStoreWriteProjection is currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT has local cache/fallback/edit-buffer write provenance to surface.

rustMemorySelectionEvidenceProjection is currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can expose cached Rust memory-gateway selected/omitted evidence without recomputing selection or exporting refs/content.

rustMemoryObjectMutationGateProjection is currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can expose Rust-owned object mutation gate readiness from /memory/readiness without scanning objects or becoming mutation authority.

rustMemoryUserRevealGrantProjection is currently expected on sessionRuntimeReadiness when XT can expose Rust-owned Assistant/User reveal grant readiness from /memory/readiness without scanning user memory or becoming memory authority.
Structured section projections should be preferred over reparsing detailLines when both are available.
detailLines remain migration-friendly context and operator-readable breadcrumbs; they are not a second authority surface.
This object is an XT-side explainability projection of Supervisor review/guidance continuity across latest review note, latest guidance, and any pending-ack guidance.
It keeps ack posture, delivery mode, intervention mode, safe-point policy, rendered refs, and summaryLine machine-readable without reparsing flat detailLines.

It is continuity explainability only and must not be treated as Hub review-note truth, guidance-injection authority, interrupt authority, grant truth, or a bypass around scheduler, audit, TTL, kill-switch, or clamp.
This object is an XT-side explainability projection of Supervisor review policy posture and the current heartbeat-derived review trigger candidate.
It keeps effective trigger sets, candidate review level/run kind, queue posture, latest review-note provenance, and summaryLine machine-readable without reparsing flat detailLines.

It is review-engine explainability only and must not be treated as scheduler authority, review-note authority, guidance-injection authority, grant truth, or a bypass around Hub audit, TTL, kill-switch, clamp, or policy gates.
This object is an XT-side explainability projection of pending Supervisor guidance delivery timing inside the current Project AI safe-point loop.
It keeps pending guidance posture, live flow location, prompt visibility, delivery state, and tool-batch pause posture machine-readable without reparsing flat detailLines.
It is explainability only and must not be treated as scheduler authority, interrupt authority, grant truth, or a bypass around Hub audit, TTL, kill-switch, clamp, or policy gates.
This object is an XT-side explainability projection of remote snapshot cache provenance for either Project AI or Supervisor memory assembly.
It exists so source truth and generic export surfaces can reuse cache scope, cache age, and TTL-left facts without reparsing detailLines.
It is provenance only and must not be treated as durable memory source truth, export approval, grant truth, or permission to bypass fail-closed routing.
This object is an XT-side projection of project effective skill capability truth and per-skill typed readiness truth for doctor/export surfaces.
It keeps the effective profile snapshot, runnable/grant-required/approval-required/blocked counts, and representative preview items machine-readable.
It is explainability and diagnostics only; it must not be treated as a grant override token, runtime bypass, or a replacement for Supervisor preflight and tool runtime policy.
This object is a compact XT-side preview of a governed skill's typed readiness state.
It exists so doctor/export consumers can see why a representative skill is blocked, waiting on grant, or waiting on local approval without reparsing ad hoc text.
This object is a presentation-friendly projection of XT project context assembly diagnostics.
It must not be treated as grant truth, policy truth, or a replacement for normalized section state.
This object is a first-class XT projection of Hub-provided prompt assembly explainability for the latest governed coding turn.
It must directly reuse Hub Generate.done metadata rather than locally inferring what prompt content XT thinks was assembled.
It is explainability only and must not be treated as prompt authority, grant truth, export approval, route truth, or a bypass around X-Constitution, audit, kill-switch, TTL, or remote export gates.
This object is an XT-side explainability projection of the latest real remote provider-key routing decision observed for a requested model.
It exists so doctor/export/troubleshoot surfaces can replay selected-account, skip-reason, and retry-window truth without locally re-running scheduler logic.
It is explainability only and must not be treated as a scheduler command, grant authority, auth scope grant, or permission to bypass Hub health, TTL, audit, kill-switch, or policy gates.
This object is a compact XT-side replay of one candidate account considered by the latest provider-key routing decision.
availability reuses the Swift Codable enum payload verbatim so doctor/export consumers can distinguish ready, cooldown, blocked, disabled, and stale candidates without reparsing free-form text.
It is diagnostic evidence only and must not be treated as direct permission to pick, unblock, or re-enable a key outside the Hub-owned scheduler/runtime flow.
This object is an XT-side explainability envelope for model-route troubleshooting surfaces that need one stable provider-key route context instead of reverse-parsing scattered detailLines.
It keeps requested model identity, any live provider-pool snapshot, the latest real selection decision, and normalized import-source blockers machine-readable in one payload.

It is explainability only and must not be treated as scheduler authority, provider-key import mutation authority, auth approval, or permission to bypass Hub health, TTL, audit, kill-switch, or policy gates.
This object is a normalized XT-side projection of one provider-key import-source blocker.
It exists so doctor/troubleshoot/export consumers can point users at the right Provider Key repair surface without reconstructing error fields from flat detailLines.
It is explainability only and must not be treated as direct import execution authority or mutation permission.
This object is a first-class XT projection of the A-tier configured-vs-runtime-ready split for governed execution.
It keeps configured/effective execution tier, runtime surface posture, trusted automation state, and missing readiness reasons machine-readable.
It is explainability only and must not be treated as Hub grant truth, tool-policy authority, or a bypass around TTL, kill-switch, clamp, or audit chains.
This object is a first-class XT projection of Project AI role-aware memory policy truth.
It keeps configured/recommended/effective project dialogue and context depth plus the A-tier memory ceiling machine-readable.
It is diagnostic and explainability oriented only; it must not be treated as grant truth, runtime authority, or a replacement for Hub policy/clamp/TTL boundaries.
This object is a first-class XT projection of Project AI memory assembly readiness as observed from recent runtime usage and explainability drift checks.
It exists so doctor and heartbeat explainability surfaces can consume Project AI memory continuity and projection-drift signals without reparsing free-form detail lines.
It is diagnostic and explainability only; it must not be treated as Hub durable truth, grant truth, or permission to bypass fail-closed memory routing.
This object is a first-class XT projection of the effective role-aware memory assembly resolution.
selected_slots, selected_planes, selected_serving_objects, and excluded_blocks exist so doctor/export surfaces do not have to infer effective assembly shape from unstructured text.
It is explainability only and must not be treated as an execution grant, scheduler command, or policy bypass surface.
This object is a first-class XT projection of Supervisor role-aware review-memory policy truth.
It keeps configured/recommended/effective Recent Raw Context and Review Memory Depth plus the S-tier review-memory ceiling machine-readable.
It is diagnostic and explainability oriented only; it must not be treated as extra runtime permission or as a replacement for Hub grant/policy/clamp/TTL boundaries.
This object is a cadence explainability projection for heartbeat-governed review, not a scheduler authority surface.
configuredSeconds, recommendedSeconds, and effectiveSeconds keep the three-value cadence model visible on XT source truth.
This object is a read-only explainability projection for the next governance review due state.
It must not be treated as permission to bypass safe-point injection, kill-switches, or review policy gates.
This object is a structured XT projection of heartbeat-governed review truth for the active project.
It exists for review explainability only and must not override normal chat memory, project memory routing, or grant/policy truth.
Review cadence explainability must remain role-aware and must not be repurposed as the master dial for recent raw context or project context depth.
Digest visibility / reason codes and digest three-line text should remain available whenever XT surfaces why a heartbeat digest was shown or suppressed.
Attached project-memory readiness fields remain explainability-only and must not be treated as memory assembly authority.
This object is a structured XT projection of recovery-beat explainability for the active session/project slice.
It must remain diagnostic-only: recoveryDecision is not direct runtime authority and must not bypass Hub grant, runtime readiness, policy, TTL, or kill-switch boundaries.
queuedReviewTrigger/queuedReviewLevel/queuedReviewRunKind describe follow-up governance intent only and must not be misread as immediate intervention execution.
This object must directly reuse the frozen upstream Diagnostic-First Route Surface rather than recomputing route truth locally.
projectionSource and completeness must remain visible wherever the projection is exported or summarized.
This object is a read-only XT projection of supervisor durable-candidate mirror evidence.
It must not be treated as proof of durable promotion, canonical Hub write success, or read-source cutover completion.
This object is an XT-local provenance snapshot showing which local cache/fallback/edit-buffer write path most recently touched each store surface.
It must not be treated as durable writer authority, canonical Hub save success, or proof that XT local storage is the long-term source of truth.
This object is a content-free XT projection of Rust /memory/readiness.object_store.mutation_gate.

It exposes mutation-control availability, confirmation posture, tombstone delete mode, immutable fail-closed behavior, and production-authority-change posture without listing memory IDs, event IDs, project IDs, prompt text, context text, or object content.
It must not be treated as permission to mutate memory; actual archive/delete/pin/unpin authority remains the Rust mutation gate and its confirmation/policy checks.
This object is a content-free XT projection of Rust /memory/readiness.object_store.user_reveal_grant.

It exposes Assistant/User reveal grant availability, TTL bounds, user-only scope, surface label, and authority-change posture without listing memory IDs, event IDs, project IDs, prompt text, context text, or user memory content.

It must not be treated as permission to reveal memory; actual issue/evaluate/revoke authority remains the Rust user-reveal grant gate, and Project Coder/model-context serving remains disallowed by default.
This object is a content-free XT projection of cached Rust memory-gateway model-call-plan selection evidence.

It may expose counts, reason buckets, layer/source aggregates, safety booleans, and bounded chunk-shape samples, but it must not export full memory IDs, chunk refs, project IDs, prompt text, context text, policy/provenance blobs, or object content.
It must not recompute memory selection and must not be treated as durable memory truth, model-serving authority, export approval, or Swift memory authority.
Route snapshot must reflect the current XT pairing/runtime path honestly and should not fabricate reachability.
